$comment: c42 D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN (C 6.5.2.1): a NEGATIVE array subscript p[-n] must step -n*sizeof(*p) bytes. The 32-bit Gep index reached the 64-bit lea/ADD un-widened, so a negative offset zero-extended to a +4GiB address -> SIGSEGV. Fix: lowerGep sign/zero-extends a sub-64-bit runtime index before the lea. The bug is DEBUG-ONLY and BASELINE is the witness: in release ConstFold folds a constant stride Mul to a negative DISPLACEMENT, which x86 encodes as a hardware-sign-extended `lea [base+disp32]` (correct, masking the bug) but arm64 CANNOT encode (no negative-disp lea variant — a PRE-EXISTING gap, deferred D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA / c43). So this corpus runs BASELINE on all 4 targets (the runtime variable-subscript cases exercise the widen via the register path on every target, incl. arm64 SXTW under qemu); NO optimizedPipelines arm is declared because a global ConstFold arm would emit a negative-disp lea that fails to compile on arm64. Strides 1 (char*), 4 (int*), 12 (struct, non-pow2), each constant + runtime. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the lowerGep widen -> crash (non-42) on the first runtime-Mul subscript in debug. Exit 42 iff all 8 sub-cases pass. sqlite uses p[-1] lookback -> run-green-critical.
